categories.title: Emotional Fine-Grained Control
categories.description: Given the same neutral text and an emotional speech prompt, the Refiner produces speech that simultaneously realizes the target emotion and applies fine-grained prosodic edits (word-level stress or pause insertion).
categories.text: Listen closely, the same words can tell a very different story.

categories.title: Refinement with Instruction
categories.description: The AudioLLM Judge diagnoses prosodic defects in the initial utterance and generates a structured refine instruction. The Refiner then corrects the speech accordingly, preserving speaker identity.

samples.text: I've told you 100 times—don't open the door at night.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion. Specifically, stress the word '100', insert a pause after 'times', stress the word 'don't', and stress the word 'night'.

samples.text: The car door slammed shut, punctuating the end of the argument.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'slammed', stress the word 'shut', and stress the word 'end', insert a pause after 'end'.

samples.text: I don't just understand you, Anne,' he said hesitatingly. 'You appear to be vexed about something.
samples.instruction: Specifically, stress the word 'understand', stress the word 'vexed', and insert a pause after 'Anne'.

samples.text: You've got to see this—it's even better than we imagined!
samples.instruction: Speak with a Happy emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'got', insert a pause after 'this', stress the word 'even', and stress the word 'better'.

samples.text: You expect me to eat this after seeing how it's prepared?
samples.instruction: Speak with a Disgusted emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'You', stress the word 'this', insert a pause after 'this', and stress the word 'prepared'.

samples.text: Upon trying her hand at painting, she discovered a natural talent within her.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Surprised emotion at a slow speed. Specifically, stress the word 'Upon', stress the word 'painting', stress the word 'discovered', and stress the word 'talent'.

samples.text: The letter slipped from his grasp, a paper cut of bitter news.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'slipped', insert a pause after 'grasp', and stress the word 'bitter'.

categories.title: Refinement with Speed & Pitch
categories.description: Beyond emotion and word-level prosodic edits, the Refiner also corrects global prosodic attributes such as speaking speed and pitch, producing speech that jointly satisfies emotion, stress, pause, speed, and pitch requirements.

samples.text: You promised you'd come back but look where we are now!
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'You', stress the word 'promised', insert a pause after 'back', stress the word 'look', and stress the word 'now'.

samples.text: I know i messed up, but please don't leave—please.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'i', insert a pause after 'up', stress the word 'please', and insert a pause after 'leave'.

samples.text: You bought a car without telling me first? That's... A bit unexpected.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Surprised emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'bought', stress the word 'first?', and insert a pause after 'That's...'.

samples.text: We danced under the stars, losing ourselves in sheer delight.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Happy emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'danced', stress the word 'stars', insert a pause after 'stars', stress the word 'sheer', and stress the word 'delight'.

samples.text: Do you really believe your silence is more tolerable than an honest mistake?
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'really', stress the word 'silence', fix the stress of tolerable, and stress the word 'honest'.

samples.text: That room smells as though something curled up and gave up half a life.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Disgusted emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'smells', stress the word 'curled', insert a pause after 'up', and stress the word 'half'.

samples.text: I—i swear i wasn't alone down there! You have to believe me this time.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion. Specifically, insert a pause after 'I—i', stress the word 'swear', stress the word 'wasn't', insert a pause after 'there!', and stress the word 'have'.
